title: Share of primary schools with toilets
subtitle: Share of public [primary](#dod:primary-education) schools equipped with with separate girls and boys sanitation facilities or toilets on the location of the educational institution.
note: Sanitation facilities or toilets include a pit latrine, an improved pit latrine, a flush toilet, a pour-flush toilet or a composting toilet.
caption:
Share of primary schools with toilets

Share of public primary schools equipped with with separate girls and boys sanitation facilities or toilets on the location of the educational institution.

This choropleth world map displays the share of public primary schools equipped with separate girls and boys sanitation facilities or toilets on the location of the educational institution in 2024. The legend spans from 0% to 100%, with tick points at every 10% interval. The darkest red shades, indicating values below 20%, are concentrated in parts of West and Central Africa: Guinea, Sierra Leone, Liberia, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo fall into this lowest visible band. Surrounding countries in the region, including Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger, appear in slightly lighter red shades that sit between 20% and 40%. In South Asia, Afghanistan and Pakistan sit in mid-range orange shades that fall between 40% and 60%. Southeast Asia shows variation: Indonesia and the Philippines appear in darker orange shades near the 50% to 70% range, while Thailand and Vietnam sit in lighter yellow-orange shades above 70%. Most of Latin America, Eastern Europe, and Central Asia show mid-to-lighter shades, generally between 50% and 80%. North America, Western Europe, Australia, and Japan sit predominantly in the lightest shades of the legend, above 90%. Large portions of South America, including Brazil and Argentina, along with Russia and China, are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data. Sanitation facilities or toilets include a pit latrine, an improved pit latrine, a flush toilet, a pour-flush toilet or a composting toilet. The visual pattern shows the lowest shares concentrated across West and Central Africa and parts of South Asia, with the highest shares distributed across much of North America, Western Europe, and Oceania.
